key_insight: A one-step flow-matching model in latent space can generate high-resolution path-dependent plastic stress fields from loading history and geometry.
engineering_relevance: This offers a fast surrogate for finite element path-dependent simulations, reducing compute cost while preserving field fidelity for complex mechanics problems.
method: Transformer-based flow matching model operating in VAE latent space
Treats plastic field prediction as video synthesis over all time steps
Adds non-Gaussian source distribution, token-level loading embeddings, and auxiliary networks
evidence_signal: The abstract reports accurate high-resolution generation with limited training data and speedups of 6–7x over FEM on CPUs and about two orders of magnitude on consumer GPUs.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the representativeness of the limited training set and on faithful encoding of complex loading paths and geometries.
paper_title: One-Step Flow Matching for Generative Modeling of Path-Dependent Physical Fields
